on_campus.name: Center Table
on_campus.description: North campus dining hall with multiple stations. Rotating menus, dedicated vegetarian/vegan options daily. Use NetNutrition to check allergens.
on_campus.notes: Newest dining hall. Has labeled dietary stations. ~40% of menu items are vegetarian/vegan.

on_campus.name: Local Point
on_campus.description: West campus dining hall. Global Kitchen station rotates cuisines. Plate station has weekly rotation but students report repetitiveness.
on_campus.notes: Longer hours than Center Table. Students note online menus don't always match what's served.

on_campus.name: Cultivate
on_campus.description: West campus gastro pub. Sit-down restaurant with a more upscale feel. Limited vegetarian options compared to dining halls.
on_campus.notes: Pricier than dining halls. Good for a nicer meal.

on_campus.name: Husky Den Food Court
on_campus.description: Food court in the HUB with multiple vendors including DUB Street Burgers. Some vegetarian options but limited dietary labeling.
on_campus.notes: Central location. Good for a quick bite between classes.

on_campus.name: District Market, Alder
on_campus.description: Neighborhood grocery store with fresh produce, grab-and-go meals, snacks, and household items. Also has Husky Grind coffee.
on_campus.notes: Good for stocking up on packaged foods with clear ingredient labels. Has ReadyStation kiosk for cash-to-card.

on_campus.name: District Market, Oak
on_campus.description: Second neighborhood grocery location near Denny Field. Same offerings as Alder location.
on_campus.notes: Also has Husky Grind café inside.

off_campus.name: Aladdin Gyro-Cery
off_campus.description: All-halal Mediterranean spot. Gyros, falafel, shawarma, famous Aladdin Fries. Open super late. Student staple since 1993.
off_campus.notes: One of the best late-night options. Always packed. Huge portions for the price.

off_campus.name: Taste of India
off_campus.description: North and South Indian cuisine with extensive vegetarian menu. Naan, curries, biryani, and dosa options.
off_campus.notes: Large vegetarian section. Good for a sit-down meal.

off_campus.name: Cedars Restaurant
off_campus.description: Indian and Mediterranean fusion. Known for tandoori, falafel, hummus, and a large vegetarian selection. Has been in the U-District for 25+ years.
off_campus.notes: Slightly further north on Brooklyn Ave. Good for groups.

off_campus.name: Araya's Place
off_campus.description: Seattle's first all-vegan Thai restaurant, open since 1987. Entirely plant-based menu. Pad thai, curries, stir fries all made vegan.
off_campus.notes: 100% vegan. One of the best-rated vegan spots in Seattle.

off_campus.name: Burger and Kabob Hut
off_campus.description: Halal burgers, kabobs, and shakes. Has vegetarian burger options including paneer masala burger. Alcohol-free.
off_campus.notes: Certified halal with sign posted. Budget-friendly.

off_campus.name: Shawarma King
off_campus.description: Egyptian and Mediterranean cuisine. Shawarma, falafel, gyros, kabab. Halal meat available.

off_campus.name: Jewel of India
off_campus.description: Affordable Indian restaurant with large vegetarian selection. Curries, rice dishes, naan, and combo platters.

off_campus.name: Thai Tom
off_campus.description: Tiny, legendary Thai spot. Wok-fired dishes made right in front of you. Vegetable pad thai and tofu stir fries available.
off_campus.notes: Cash only. Always a line. Tiny space but worth the wait.

off_campus.name: Xi'an Noodles
off_campus.description: Hand-pulled noodles and Chinese street food. Has vegetarian noodle and rice options.
off_campus.notes: The hand-pulled noodles are the move. Some veggie options but menu is mostly meat-based.

off_campus.name: Chipotle
off_campus.description: Fast casual Mexican chain. Sofritas (tofu), veggie bowls, and customizable options. All ingredients clearly labeled online.
off_campus.notes: Reliable option when you need something quick and customizable. Allergen info available online.

off_campus.name: Broadfork Café
off_campus.description: Breakfast and lunch café with salads, wraps, grain bowls. Lots of vegan and gluten-free options clearly marked.
off_campus.notes: Good for a healthy breakfast or lunch. Closes at 3pm.

off_campus.name: Guanaco's Tacos Pupuseria
off_campus.description: Salvadoran and Mexican food. Bean and cheese pupusas, veggie tacos. Affordable and filling.
off_campus.notes: Vegetarian pupusas are excellent. Cash-friendly prices.

off_campus.name: Veggie Grill by Next Level
off_campus.description: All plant-based restaurant chain. Veggie burgers, sandwiches, salads, bowls. Entirely vegan menu.
off_campus.notes: 100% vegan. Good option if you want something that feels like 'normal' fast food but plant-based.

off_campus.name: Memo's Mexican Food
off_campus.description: Bean and cheese burritos, veggie quesadillas. Classic cheap Mexican food near campus.
off_campus.notes: Solid budget pick. Not many vegan options but vegetarian is easy here.

off_campus.name: Café on the Ave
off_campus.description: Cheap Thai-inspired bowls with tofu and vegetable options. Pad thai, curries, rice plates.
off_campus.notes: Very affordable. Good for a quick lunch.

metadata.area: University of Washington Seattle / University District
metadata.notes: GPS coordinates are approximate based on street addresses. Verify hours before visiting — hours may change quarterly. On-campus locations accept Husky Card/dining plan only (no cash). Off-campus locations accept cash/credit.
